[[File:Mikami-jinja honden-1.JPG|thumb|right|200px|The Main Building of Mikami Shrine, a National Treasure]] '''Mikami Shrine''' ({{Lang-ja|御神神社}}, is a [[Shintoist]] [[shrine]] in [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu City, Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd rank|Kanpei-taisha]], it is located at the foot of [[Mount Mikami]] (432 meters above sea level), a prominant hill in this flat area near [[Lake Biwa]].
The shrine's legend tells that it was established when Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, came down on Mout Mikami, as its [[Shintai]], during the reign of [[Emperor Kōrei]] of the third century B.C., and that a building of worship was built by [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] in the current place. The shrine appears in the "Shrines Volume" of the "''Engishiki'' ({{Lang-ja|延喜式}}", "Procedures of the Engi Era") of the tenth century A.D.
The shrine's main building, built in the [[Kamakura Period]], is a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]]. There are other [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]] in the shrine compound. <ref> [http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] </ref> The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is a [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. <ref> [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as a Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) </ref>
<gallery> File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|The Worship Building, an Important Cultural Property (where weddings are often held) File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]] File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|An Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami </gallery>
==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]]
==References== <references/>
==External Links==
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of worship in Shiga Prefecture]]

The shrine appears in the "Shrines Volume" of the "''Engishiki'' ({{Lang-ja|延喜式}}", "Procedures of the Engi Era") of the tenth century A.D.
The shrine's main building, built in the [[Kamakura Period]], is a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]]. There are other [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]] in the shrine compound. <ref> [http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] </ref> The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is a [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. <ref> [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as a Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) </ref>

[[File:Mikami-jinja honden-1.JPG|thumb|right|200px|The Main Building of Mikami Shrine, a National Treasure]] '''Mikami Shrine''' ({{Lang-ja|御上神社}}), is a [[Shintoist]] [[shrine]] in [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu City, Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd rank|Kanpei-taisha]], it is located at the foot of [[Mount Mikami]] (432 meters above sea level), a prominent hill in this flat area near [[Lake Biwa]].
==Legend and History== The shrine's legends tell that it was established when Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, came down on Mout Mikami, as its [[Shintai]], during the reign of [[Emperor Kōrei]] of the third century B.C., and that a building of worship was built by [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] in the current place ca. 700 A.D. The shrine appears in the "Shrines Volume" of the "''Engishiki'' ({{Lang-ja|延喜式}}", "Procedures of the Engi Era") of the tenth century A.D.
==National Treasure== The shrine's main building, built in the [[Kamakura Period]], is a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]]. There are other [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]] in the shrine compound. <ref> [http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] </ref>
==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is aｎ [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. <ref> [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as a Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) </ref> The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a paddy field nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony.

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Mikami Shrine | image = Mikami-jinja honden-1.JPG | width = | caption = The Main Building of Mikami Shrine, a National Treasure | type = | dedication = | founded = | closed = | founder = | priest = | location_map = Japan | coordinates ={{coord|35.0500|N|136.0274|E|source:wikidata|display=title,inline}} | address = | phone = | website = }} '''Mikami Shrine''' ({{Lang-ja|御上神社}}) is a [[Shintoist]] [[shrine]] in [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu City, Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd rank|Kanpei-taisha]], it is located at the foot of [[Mount Mikami]] (432 meters above sea level), a prominent hill in this flat area near [[Lake Biwa]].
==Legend and History== The shrine's legends tell that it was established when Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, came down on Mout Mikami, as its [[Shintai]], during the reign of [[Emperor Kōrei]] of the third century B.C., and that a building of worship was built by [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] in the current place ca. 700 A.D. The shrine appears in the "Shrines Volume" of the "''Engishiki'' ({{Lang-ja|延喜式}}", "Procedures of the Engi Era") of the tenth century A.D.
==National Treasure== The shrine's main building, built in the [[Kamakura Period]], is a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]]. There are other [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]] in the shrine compound.<ref> [http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] </ref>
==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. <ref> [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as a Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) </ref> The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a paddy field nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony.
<gallery> File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|The Worship Building, an Important Cultural Property (where weddings are often held) File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]], as the Shrine's [[Shintai]] File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. </gallery>
==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]]
==References== <references/>
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Mikami Shrine
御上神社 | image = Mikami-jinja honden-1.JPG | caption = ''Honden'' of Mikami Shrine, (NT) | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.0500|N|136.0274|E|source:wikidata|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Mikami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto | founded_by = [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] | established = c.718 AD | date_destroyed = | location = [[Yasu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | website = {{official website|http://www.mikami-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikami Shrine'''|御上神社|Mikami Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine is Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, who in legend, descended onto 432-meter [[Mount Mikami]] during the reign of [[Emperor Kōrei]] (290 - 215 BC) to become the ''[[shintai]]'' of the mountain.[http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] ===History== The foundation of Mikami Jinja is uncertain. While it is known that [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] ordered the construction of a shrine at the current location in 718 AD, [[archaeological excavation]]s have found the that the area was connected with rituals pertaining to Mount Mikami from a much earlier period. In the [[Meiji period]], 24 ''[[dōtaku]]'' were excavated at the foot of the mountain, indicating that it was worshipped from the [[Yayoi period]]. The shrine is mentioned in several occasions in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' completed in 901 and is also mentioned in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' list of shrines in [[Ōmi Province]] dated 927 AD. In 1876, following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. It was promoted to the rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1913 and to [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd rank|''Kanpei-taisha'']], or Imperial shrine of the 2nd rank in 1924. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from [[Yasu Station]] on then [[JR West]] [[Biwako Line]]. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''Irimoya-zukuri'']], style roof,, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952. {{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Mikami Shrine
御上神社 | image = Mikami-jinja honden-1.JPG | caption = ''Honden'' of Mikami Shrine, (NT) | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.0500|N|136.0274|E|source:wikidata|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Mikami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto | founded_by = [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] | established = c.718 AD | date_destroyed = | location = [[Yasu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | website = {{official website|http://www.mikami-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikami Shrine'''|御上神社|Mikami Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from [[Yasu Station (Shiga)|Yasu Station]] on then [[JR West]] [[Biwako Line]]. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''Irimoya-zukuri'']], style roof,, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952. {{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]

御上神社 | image = Mikami-jinja honden-1.JPG | caption = ''Honden'' of Mikami Shrine, (NT) | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.0500|N|136.0274|E|source:wikidata|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Mikami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto | founded_by = [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] | established = c.718 AD | date_destroyed = | location = [[Yasu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | website = {{official website|http://www.mikami-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikami Shrine'''|御上神社|Mikami Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from [[Yasu Station (Shiga)|Yasu Station]] on then [[JR West]] [[Biwako Line]]. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''Irimoya-zukuri'']], style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952. {{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Mikami Shrine
御上神社 | image = Mikami-jinja honden-1.JPG | caption = ''Honden'' of Mikami Shrine, (NT) | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.0500|N|136.0274|E|source:wikidata|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Mikami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto | founded_by = [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] | established = c.718 AD | date_destroyed = | location = [[Yasu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | website = {{official website|http://www.mikami-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikami Shrine'''|御上神社|Mikami Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine is Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, who in legend, descended onto 432-meter [[Mount Mikami]] during the reign of [[Emperor Kōrei]] (290 - 215 BC) to become the ''[[shintai]]'' of the mountain.[http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] ==History== The foundation of Mikami Jinja is uncertain. While it is known that [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] ordered the construction of a shrine at the current location in 718 AD, [[archaeological excavation]]s have found the that the area was connected with rituals pertaining to Mount Mikami from a much earlier period. In the [[Meiji period]], 24 ''[[dōtaku]]'' were excavated at the foot of the mountain, indicating that it was worshipped from the [[Yayoi period]]. The shrine is mentioned in several occasions in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' completed in 901 and is also mentioned in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' list of shrines in [[Ōmi Province]] dated 927 AD. In 1876, following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. It was promoted to the rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1913 and to [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd rank|''Kanpei-taisha'']], or Imperial shrine of the 2nd rank in 1924. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from [[Yasu Station (Shiga)|Yasu Station]] on then [[JR West]] [[Biwako Line]]. [[Category:Beppo Shrines]] ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''Irimoya-zukuri'']], style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952. {{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]

revision.comment: Moving [[:Category:Beppo Shrines]] to [[:Category:Beppyo shrines]] per [[Wikipedia:Categories for discussion/Log/2022 June 2#Category:Beppo Shrines]]

It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Meishin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-chūsha]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Mikami Shrine
御上神社 | image = Mikami-jinja honden-1.JPG | caption = ''Honden'' of Mikami Shrine, (NT) | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.0500|N|136.0274|E|source:wikidata|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Mikami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto | founded_by = [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] | established = c.718 AD | date_destroyed = | location = [[Yasu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | website = {{official website|http://www.mikami-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikami Shrine'''|御上神社|Mikami Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine is Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, who in legend, descended onto 432-meter [[Mount Mikami]] during the reign of [[Emperor Kōrei]] (290 - 215 BC) to become the ''[[shintai]]'' of the mountain.[http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] ==History== The foundation of Mikami Jinja is uncertain. While it is known that [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] ordered the construction of a shrine at the current location in 718 AD, [[archaeological excavation]]s have found the that the area was connected with rituals pertaining to Mount Mikami from a much earlier period. In the [[Meiji period]], 24 ''[[dōtaku]]'' were excavated at the foot of the mountain, indicating that it was worshipped from the [[Yayoi period]]. The shrine is mentioned in several occasions in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' completed in 901 and is also mentioned in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' list of shrines in [[Ōmi Province]] dated 927 AD. In 1876, following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. It was promoted to the rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1913 and to [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd rank|''Kanpei-taisha'']], or Imperial shrine of the 2nd rank in 1924. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from [[Yasu Station (Shiga)|Yasu Station]] on then [[JR West]] [[Biwako Line]]. [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''Irimoya-zukuri'']], style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952. {{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-chūsha]]

It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952. {{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Mikami Shrine
御上神社 | image = Mikami-jinja honden-1.JPG | caption = ''Honden'' of Mikami Shrine, (NT) | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.0500|N|136.0274|E|source:wikidata|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Mikami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|ja|天之御影神}} | founded_by = [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] | established = c.718 AD | date_destroyed = | location = [[Yasu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | website = {{official website|http://www.mikami-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikami Shrine'''|御上神社|Mikami Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine is {{ill|Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|ja|天之御影神}}, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, who in legend, descended onto 432-meter [[Mount Mikami]] during the reign of [[Emperor Kōrei]] (290 - 215 BC) to become the ''[[shintai]]'' of the mountain.[http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] ==History== The foundation of Mikami Jinja is uncertain. While it is known that [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] ordered the construction of a shrine at the current location in 718 AD, [[archaeological excavation]]s have found that the area was connected with rituals pertaining to Mount Mikami from a much earlier period. In the [[Meiji period]], 24 ''[[dōtaku]]'' were excavated at the foot of the mountain, indicating that it was worshipped from the [[Yayoi period]]. The shrine is mentioned in several occasions in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' completed in 901 and is also mentioned in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' list of shrines in [[Ōmi Province]] dated 927 AD. In 1876, following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. It was promoted to the rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1913 and to [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd rank|''Kanpei-taisha'']], or Imperial shrine of the 2nd rank in 1924. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from [[Yasu Station (Shiga)|Yasu Station]] on then [[JR West]] [[Biwako Line]]. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''Irimoya-zukuri'']], style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]]

If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''Irimoya-zukuri'']], style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]]

It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]][[en:Mikami Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Mikami Shrine
御上神社 | image = Mikami-jinja honden-1.JPG | caption = ''Honden'' of Mikami Shrine, (NT) | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.0500|N|136.0274|E|source:wikidata|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Mikami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|ja|天之御影神}} | founded_by = [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] | established = c.718 AD | date_destroyed = | location = [[Yasu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | website = {{official website|http://www.mikami-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikami Shrine'''|御上神社|Mikami Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine is {{ill|Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|ja|天之御影神}}, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, who in legend, descended onto 432-meter [[Mount Mikami]] during the reign of [[Emperor Kōrei]] (290 - 215 BC) to become the ''[[shintai]]'' of the mountain.[http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] ==History== The foundation of Mikami Jinja is uncertain. While it is known that [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] ordered the construction of a shrine at the current location in 718 AD, [[archaeological excavation]]s have found that the area was connected with rituals pertaining to Mount Mikami from a much earlier period. In the [[Meiji period]], 24 ''[[dōtaku]]'' were excavated at the foot of the mountain, indicating that it was worshipped from the [[Yayoi period]]. The shrine is mentioned in several occasions in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' completed in 901 and is also mentioned in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' list of shrines in [[Ōmi Province]] dated 927 AD. In 1876, following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. It was promoted to the rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1913 and to [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd rank|''Kanpei-taisha'']], or Imperial shrine of the 2nd rank in 1924. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from [[Yasu Station (Shiga)|Yasu Station]] on then [[JR West]] [[Biwako Line]]. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''Irimoya-zukuri'']], style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[en:Mikami Shrine]][[Category:Shinto]][[en:Mikami Shrine]]

If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''Irimoya-zukuri'']], style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[en:Mikami Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Mikami Shrine]][[en:Mikami Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Mikami Shrine
御上神社 | image = Mikami-jinja honden-1.JPG | caption = ''Honden'' of Mikami Shrine, (NT) | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.0500|N|136.0274|E|source:wikidata|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Mikami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|ja|天之御影神}} | founded_by = [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] | established = c.718 AD | date_destroyed = | location = [[Yasu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | website = {{official website|http://www.mikami-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikami Shrine'''|御上神社|Mikami Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine is {{ill|Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|ja|天之御影神}}, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, who in legend, descended onto 432-meter [[Mount Mikami]] during the reign of [[Emperor Kōrei]] (290 - 215 BC) to become the ''[[shintai]]'' of the mountain.[http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] ==History== The foundation of Mikami Jinja is uncertain. While it is known that [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] ordered the construction of a shrine at the current location in 718 AD, [[archaeological excavation]]s have found that the area was connected with rituals pertaining to Mount Mikami from a much earlier period. In the [[Meiji period]], 24 ''[[dōtaku]]'' were excavated at the foot of the mountain, indicating that it was worshipped from the [[Yayoi period]]. The shrine is mentioned in several occasions in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' completed in 901 and is also mentioned in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' list of shrines in [[Ōmi Province]] dated 927 AD. In 1876, following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. It was promoted to the rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1913 and to [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd rank|''Kanpei-taisha'']], or Imperial shrine of the 2nd rank in 1924. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from [[Yasu Station (Shiga)|Yasu Station]] on then [[JR West]] [[Biwako Line]]. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''Irimoya-zukuri'']], style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[en:Mikami Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[en:Mikami Shrine]][[en:Mikami Shrine]]

Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from [[Yasu Station (Shiga)|Yasu Station]] on then [[JR West]] [[Biwako Line]]. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''Irimoya-zukuri'']], style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[en:Mikami Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia40]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia19]] [[en:Mikami Shrine]][[en:Mikami Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Mikami Shrine
御上神社 | image = Mikami-jinja honden-1.JPG | caption = ''Honden'' of Mikami Shrine, (NT) | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.0500|N|136.0274|E|source:wikidata|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Mikami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|ja|天之御影神}} | founded_by = [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] | established = c.718 AD | date_destroyed = | location = [[Yasu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | website = {{official website|http://www.mikami-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikami Shrine'''|御上神社|Mikami Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine is {{ill|Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|ja|天之御影神}}, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, who in legend, descended onto 432-meter [[Mount Mikami]] during the reign of [[Emperor Kōrei]] (290 - 215 BC) to become the ''[[shintai]]'' of the mountain.[http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] ==History== The foundation of Mikami Jinja is uncertain. While it is known that [[Fujiwara no Fuhito]] ordered the construction of a shrine at the current location in 718 AD, [[archaeological excavation]]s have found that the area was connected with rituals pertaining to Mount Mikami from a much earlier period. In the [[Meiji period]], 24 ''[[dōtaku]]'' were excavated at the foot of the mountain, indicating that it was worshipped from the [[Yayoi period]]. The shrine is mentioned in several occasions in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' completed in 901 and is also mentioned in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' list of shrines in [[Ōmi Province]] dated 927 AD. In 1876, following the [[Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. It was promoted to the rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1913 and to [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd rank|''Kanpei-taisha'']], or Imperial shrine of the 2nd rank in 1924. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from [[Yasu Station (Shiga)|Yasu Station]] on then [[JR West]] [[Biwako Line]]. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as [[East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''Irimoya-zukuri'']], style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[Mount Mikami]] * [[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[en:Mikami Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia40]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia19]] [[en:Mikami Shrine]][[en:Mikami Shrine]]

It was promoted to the rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1913 and to [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd rank|''Kanpei-taisha'']], or Imperial shrine of the 2nd rank in 1924. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from [[Yasu Station (Shiga)|Yasu Station]] on then [[JR West]] [[Biwako Line]]. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details.
