[[commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] [[commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11488762]] Wikidata name: Mikami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Mikami Shrine
御上神社 | image = File:Mikami-jinja keidai-2.JPG | caption = ''Honden'' of Mikami Shrine, (NT) | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.05|N|136.027361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Mikami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}} | founded_by = [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] | established = c.718 AD | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Yasu, Shiga|Yasu, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | website = {{official website|http://www.mikami-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} | location = {{ill|Yasu|WD=Q611642}} | part_of = {{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|WD=Q10444949}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikami Shrine'''|御上神社|Mikami Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine is {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}}, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, who in legend, descended onto 432-meter [[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] (290 - 215 BC) to become the ''[[shintai]]'' of the mountain.[http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] ==History== The foundation of Mikami Jinja is uncertain. While it is known that [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] ordered the construction of a shrine at the current location in 718 AD, [[archaeological excavation]]s have found that the area was connected with rituals pertaining to Mount Mikami from a much earlier period. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], 24 ''[[:en:Dōtaku|Dōtaku]]'' were excavated at the foot of the mountain, indicating that it was worshipped from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]. The shrine is mentioned in several occasions in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' completed in 901 and is also mentioned in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' list of shrines in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]] dated 927 AD. In 1876, following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. It was promoted to the Rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1913 and to [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd Rank|''Kanpei-taisha'']], or Imperial shrine of the 2nd Rank in 1924. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from [[:en:Yasu Station (Shiga)|Yasu Station]] on then [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[:en:Kamakura Period|Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''Irimoya-zukuri'']], style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[:en:List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]] * [[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Mikami Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} [[en:Mikami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Mikami Jinja}} [[ja:御上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488762}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[:en:List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]] * [[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Mikami Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} [[en:Mikami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Mikami Jinja}} [[ja:御上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488762}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

[[commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] [[commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11488762]] Wikidata name: Mikami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Mikami Shrine
御上神社 | image = File:Mikami-jinja keidai-2.JPG | caption = ''Honden'' of Mikami Shrine, (NT) | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.05|N|136.027361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Mikami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}} | founded_by = [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] | established = c.718 AD | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Yasu, Shiga|Yasu, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | website = {{official website|http://www.mikami-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} | location = {{ill|Yasu|WD=Q611642}} | part_of = {{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|WD=Q10444949}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikami Shrine'''|御上神社|Mikami Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine is {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}}, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, who in legend, descended onto 432-meter [[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] (290 - 215 BC) to become the ''[[shintai]]'' of the mountain.[http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] ==History== The foundation of Mikami Jinja is uncertain. While it is known that [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] ordered the construction of a shrine at the current location in 718 AD, [[archaeological excavation]]s have found that the area was connected with rituals pertaining to Mount Mikami from a much earlier period. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], 24 ''[[:en:Dōtaku|Dōtaku]]'' were excavated at the foot of the mountain, indicating that it was worshipped from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]. The shrine is mentioned in several occasions in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' completed in 901 and is also mentioned in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' list of shrines in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]] dated 927 AD. In 1876, following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. It was promoted to the Rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1913 and to [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd Rank|''Kanpei-taisha'']], or Imperial shrine of the 2nd Rank in 1924. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from [[:en:Yasu Station (Shiga)|Yasu Station]] on then [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[:en:Kamakura Period|Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''Irimoya-zukuri'']], style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[:en:List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]] * [[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Mikami Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Mikami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Mikami Jinja}} [[ja:御上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488762}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''Irimoya-zukuri'']], style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[:en:List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]] * [[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Mikami Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Mikami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Mikami Jinja}} [[ja:御上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488762}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]]

revision.comment: Bot: categorize pages with unclosed brackets
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11488762]] Wikidata name: Mikami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Mikami Shrine
御上神社 | image = File:Mikami-jinja keidai-2.JPG | caption = ''Honden'' of Mikami Shrine, (NT) | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.05|N|136.027361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Mikami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}} | founded_by = [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] | established = c.718 AD | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Yasu, Shiga|Yasu, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | website = {{official website|http://www.mikami-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} | location = {{ill|Yasu|WD=Q611642}} | part_of = {{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|WD=Q10444949}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikami Shrine'''|御上神社|Mikami Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine is {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}}, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, who in legend, descended onto 432-meter [[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] (290 - 215 BC) to become the ''[[shintai]]'' of the mountain.[http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] ==History== The foundation of Mikami Jinja is uncertain. While it is known that [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] ordered the construction of a shrine at the current location in 718 AD, [[archaeological excavation]]s have found that the area was connected with rituals pertaining to Mount Mikami from a much earlier period. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], 24 ''[[:en:Dōtaku|Dōtaku]]'' were excavated at the foot of the mountain, indicating that it was worshipped from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]. The shrine is mentioned in several occasions in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' completed in 901 and is also mentioned in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' list of shrines in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]] dated 927 AD. In 1876, following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. It was promoted to the Rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1913 and to [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd Rank|''Kanpei-taisha'']], or Imperial shrine of the 2nd Rank in 1924. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from [[:en:Yasu Station (Shiga)|Yasu Station]] on then [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[:en:Kamakura Period|Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|''Irimoya-zukuri'']], style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]]|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an [[:en:List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]] nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|[[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]], as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * [[:en:Mount Mikami|Mount Mikami]] * [[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Mikami Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Mikami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Mikami Jinja}} [[ja:御上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488762}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

It was promoted to the Rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1913 and to [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd Rank|''Kanpei-taisha'']], or Imperial shrine of the 2nd Rank in 1924. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from {{ill|Yasu Station (Shiga)|en|Yasu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q1042474|lt=Yasu Station}} on then {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line}}. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[:en:Kamakura Period|Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=''Irimoya-zukuri''}}, style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}} in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|{{ill|Rōmon|en|Rōmon|WD=Q3458957|lt=Rōmon}}|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an {{ill|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|en|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|WD=Q111360896|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}.[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Emperor Showa}}'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}} nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|{{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}}, as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * {{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}} * {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Mikami Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Mikami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Mikami Jinja}} [[ja:御上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488762}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

[[co:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11488762]] Wikidata name: Mikami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Mikami Shrine
御上神社 | image = File:Mikami-jinja keidai-2.JPG | caption = ''Honden'' of Mikami Shrine, (NT) | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.05|N|136.027361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Mikami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}} | founded_by = {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} | established = c.718 AD | date_destroyed = | location = {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|WD=Q611642|lt=Yasu, Shiga}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | website = {{official website|http://www.mikami-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}}} | location = {{ill|Yasu|WD=Q611642}} | part_of = {{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|WD=Q10444949}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikami Shrine'''|御上神社|Mikami Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|WD=Q611642|lt=Yasu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine is {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}}, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, who in legend, descended onto 432-meter {{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} (290 - 215 BC) to become the ''[[shintai]]'' of the mountain.[http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] ==History== The foundation of Mikami Jinja is uncertain. While it is known that {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} ordered the construction of a shrine at the current location in 718 AD, [[archaeological excavation]]s have found that the area was connected with rituals pertaining to Mount Mikami from a much earlier period. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, 24 ''{{ill|Dōtaku|en|Dōtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=Dōtaku}}'' were excavated at the foot of the mountain, indicating that it was worshipped from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}. The shrine is mentioned in several occasions in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' completed in 901 and is also mentioned in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' list of shrines in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}} dated 927 AD. In 1876, following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}}, the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. It was promoted to the Rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1913 and to [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd Rank|''Kanpei-taisha'']], or Imperial shrine of the 2nd Rank in 1924. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from {{ill|Yasu Station (Shiga)|en|Yasu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q1042474|lt=Yasu Station}} on then {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line}}. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[:en:Kamakura Period|Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=''Irimoya-zukuri''}}, style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}} in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|{{ill|Rōmon|en|Rōmon|WD=Q3458957|lt=Rōmon}}|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an {{ill|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|en|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|WD=Q111360896|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}.[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Emperor Showa}}'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}} nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|{{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}}, as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * {{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}} * {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category}} *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - [[co:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11488762]] Wikidata name: Mikami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Mikami Shrine
御上神社 | image = File:Mikami-jinja keidai-2.JPG | caption = ''Honden'' of Mikami Shrine, (NT) | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.05|N|136.027361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Mikami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}} | founded_by = {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} | established = c.718 AD | date_destroyed = | location = {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|WD=Q611642|lt=Yasu, Shiga}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | website = {{official website|http://www.mikami-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}}} | location = {{ill|Yasu|WD=Q611642}} | part_of = {{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|WD=Q10444949}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikami Shrine'''|御上神社|Mikami Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|WD=Q611642|lt=Yasu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine is {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}}, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, who in legend, descended onto 432-meter {{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} (290 - 215 BC) to become the ''[[shintai]]'' of the mountain.[http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] ==History== The foundation of Mikami Jinja is uncertain. While it is known that {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} ordered the construction of a shrine at the current location in 718 AD, [[archaeological excavation]]s have found that the area was connected with rituals pertaining to Mount Mikami from a much earlier period. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, 24 ''{{ill|Dōtaku|en|Dōtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=Dōtaku}}'' were excavated at the foot of the mountain, indicating that it was worshipped from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}. The shrine is mentioned in several occasions in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' completed in 901 and is also mentioned in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' list of shrines in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}} dated 927 AD. In 1876, following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}}, the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. It was promoted to the Rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1913 and to [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd Rank|''Kanpei-taisha'']], or Imperial shrine of the 2nd Rank in 1924. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from {{ill|Yasu Station (Shiga)|en|Yasu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q1042474|lt=Yasu Station}} on then {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line}}. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[:en:Kamakura Period|Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=''Irimoya-zukuri''}}, style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}} in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|{{ill|Rōmon|en|Rōmon|WD=Q3458957|lt=Rōmon}}|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an {{ill|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|en|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|WD=Q111360896|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}.[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Emperor Showa}}'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}} nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|{{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}}, as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * {{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}} * {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category:Category]] *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Mikami Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Mikami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Mikami Jinja}} [[ja:御上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488762}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}} in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|{{ill|Rōmon|en|Rōmon|WD=Q3458957|lt=Rōmon}}|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an {{ill|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|en|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|WD=Q111360896|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}.[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Emperor Showa}}'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}} nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|{{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}}, as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * {{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}} * {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category:Category]] *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Mikami Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Mikami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Mikami Jinja}} [[ja:御上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488762}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]

[[co:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Mikami Shrine
御上神社 | image = File:Mikami-jinja keidai-2.JPG | caption = ''Honden'' of Mikami Shrine, (NT) | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.05|N|136.027361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Mikami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}} | founded_by = {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} | established = c.718 AD | date_destroyed = | location = {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|WD=Q611642|lt=Yasu, Shiga}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | website = {{official website|http://www.mikami-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}}} | location = {{ill|Yasu|WD=Q611642}} | part_of = {{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|WD=Q10444949}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikami Shrine'''|御上神社|Mikami Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|WD=Q611642|lt=Yasu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine is {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}}, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, who in legend, descended onto 432-meter {{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} (290 - 215 BC) to become the ''[[shintai]]'' of the mountain.[http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] ==History== The foundation of Mikami Jinja is uncertain. While it is known that {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} ordered the construction of a shrine at the current location in 718 AD, [[archaeological excavation]]s have found that the area was connected with rituals pertaining to Mount Mikami from a much earlier period. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, 24 ''{{ill|Dōtaku|en|Dōtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=Dōtaku}}'' were excavated at the foot of the mountain, indicating that it was worshipped from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}. The shrine is mentioned in several occasions in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' completed in 901 and is also mentioned in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' list of shrines in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}} dated 927 AD. In 1876, following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}}, the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. It was promoted to the Rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1913 and to [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd Rank|''Kanpei-taisha'']], or Imperial shrine of the 2nd Rank in 1924. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from {{ill|Yasu Station (Shiga)|en|Yasu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q1042474|lt=Yasu Station}} on then {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line}}. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[:en:Kamakura Period|Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=''Irimoya-zukuri''}}, style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}} in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|{{ill|Rōmon|en|Rōmon|WD=Q3458957|lt=Rōmon}}|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an {{ill|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|en|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|WD=Q111360896|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}.[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Emperor Showa}}'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}} nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|{{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}}, as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * {{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}} * {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category:Category]] *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - [[co:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Mikami Jinja]][[en:Mikami Shrine]][[ja:御上神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Mikami Shrine
御上神社 | image = File:Mikami-jinja keidai-2.JPG | caption = ''Honden'' of Mikami Shrine, (NT) | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.05|N|136.027361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Mikami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}} | founded_by = {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} | established = c.718 AD | date_destroyed = | location = {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|WD=Q611642|lt=Yasu, Shiga}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | website = {{official website|http://www.mikami-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}}} | location = {{ill|Yasu|WD=Q611642}} | part_of = {{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|WD=Q10444949}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikami Shrine'''|御上神社|Mikami Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|WD=Q611642|lt=Yasu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The ''[[kami]]'' worshipped at this shrine is {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}}, [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson, who in legend, descended onto 432-meter {{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} (290 - 215 BC) to become the ''[[shintai]]'' of the mountain.[http://en.biwako-visitors.jp/gobiwako/attractions/index.php?act=dtl&id=24 Mikami Shrine] ==History== The foundation of Mikami Jinja is uncertain. While it is known that {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} ordered the construction of a shrine at the current location in 718 AD, [[archaeological excavation]]s have found that the area was connected with rituals pertaining to Mount Mikami from a much earlier period. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, 24 ''{{ill|Dōtaku|en|Dōtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=Dōtaku}}'' were excavated at the foot of the mountain, indicating that it was worshipped from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}. The shrine is mentioned in several occasions in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' completed in 901 and is also mentioned in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' list of shrines in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}} dated 927 AD. In 1876, following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}}, the shrine was officially designated a “county shrine”. It was promoted to the Rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1913 and to [[Kanpei-taisha#Imperial shrines, 2nd Rank|''Kanpei-taisha'']], or Imperial shrine of the 2nd Rank in 1924. Mikami Jinja is a ten-minute drive from {{ill|Yasu Station (Shiga)|en|Yasu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q1042474|lt=Yasu Station}} on then {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line}}. ==Cultural Properties== ; {{nihongo|[[Honden]]|本殿||}}: The shrine's main building is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the [[:en:Kamakura Period|Kamakura Period]] from its various stylistic details. If fuses Shinto shrine architecture with Buddhist elements such as {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=''Irimoya-zukuri''}}, style roof, plaster walls, and "Renko" windows. It was declared a protected building as early as 1899, but was formally designated as a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}} in 1952.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1440|title=御上神社本殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Honden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]|拝殿||}}: Dating from then later Kamukura period, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} (ICP) in 1899.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1441|title= 御上神社拝殿 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Haiden |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ; {{nihongo|{{ill|Rōmon|en|Rōmon|WD=Q3458957|lt=Rōmon}}|楼門||}}: Dating from the late Kamakura period, it was designated as an ICP in 1899{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1442|title= 御上神社楼門 |trans-title= Mikami Shrine Romon |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ;{{nihongo|Wakamiya Jinja Honden|摂社若宮神社本殿||}}: Belonging to a subsidiary shrine within the same compound, this structure dates from the late Muromachi period. It is designated as an ICP in 1931{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1443|title=御上神社摂社若宮神社本殿|trans-title= Mikami Shrine subsidiary Wakamiya Shrine Main Hal; |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==Festivals== The "Zuiki Festival of Mikami", celebrated every October as a harvesting festival, is an {{ill|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|en|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|WD=Q111360896|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}.[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215239 The Zuiki Festival as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property] (in Japanese) The rice for {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Emperor Showa}}'s first [[Labor Thanksgiving Day#History|Niiname Festival]] in 1925 was grown in a {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}} nearby, which is celebrated every year in June by a rice planting ceremony. ==Gallery== File:Mikami-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Haiden, ICP Wakamiya-jinja (Mikami-jinja).JPG|Wakamiya Jinja, ICP Mikami Jinja Rōmon-2.JPG| Rōmon, ICP File:Mikamiyama.jpg|{{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}}, as the shrine's ''[[shintai]]'' File:Mikami-jinja_okumiya.JPG|The Outer Shrine on top of Mount Mikami File:Mikami-jinja yuukisaiden.JPG|The rice for Emperor Showa was grown here. ==See also== * {{ill|Mount Mikami|en|Mount Mikami|WD=Q10865239|lt=Mount Mikami}} * {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] *[http://www.mikami-jinja.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/180/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Mikami Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Mikami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Mikami Jinja}} [[ja:御上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488762}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yasu, Shiga]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]
